# Frontend Performance Standards

Every FE module generated by the scaffold already applies the following optimizations by default.
Do NOT override or revert to old patterns (IIFE + single bundle + no cache).

## Code Splitting
- **esbuild `format: 'esm'`** + `splitting: true` — entry point di-split jadi chunk terpisah.
- **React/ReactDOM externalized** — not bundled per module, served as a shared vendor chunk.
- HTML templates load vendor via `<script src="/static/vendor/react.production.min.js">` lalu entry via `<script type="module">`.

## Tree Shaking
- `treeShaking: true` di esbuild config.
- Dead code is automatically dropped during production build.

## Asset Caching (Go Router)
- `dist/` assets → `Cache-Control: public, max-age=31536000, immutable` (fingerprinted).
- `vendor/` → `Cache-Control: public, max-age=31536000, immutable`.
- `css/` → `Cache-Control: public, max-age=3600`.

## API Cache (Client-side)
- `api.ts` has an in-memory cache with stale-while-revalidate pattern.
- GET responses are cached for 10 seconds, returned instantly when stale while refreshing in the background.
- Request deduplication — identical fetches within the same timeframe only make 1 request.
- Mutations (POST/PUT/DELETE) automatically bust related caches.

## Component Memoization
- **`React.memo`** for list item cards — prevents re-render when parent state changes.
- **`useCallback`** for event handlers — stable reference across renders.
- **`useMemo`** for derived values (date formatting, post count).
### SSR Skeleton HTML
- Server renders simplified HTML (skeleton) from data before React loads — user sees content instantly.
- React uses **`createRoot`** — replaces skeleton with full component (avoids hydration mismatch).
- Graceful degradation: if server fetch fails, React fetches client-side via `api.ts`.
## Bundle Analysis
- Production build automatically generates `metafile` — run `npm run build -w modules/<feature>` and check the analysis output.
- `--analyze` flag for verbose breakdown.

## Font & Resource Hints
- `<link rel="preload">` for vendor scripts in `<head>`.
- Critical CSS inline via `<style>` — body fade-in.

> When adding a new module, do NOT write FE boilerplate manually.
> Scaffold already includes all the optimizations above.
